BAILEY-BOUSHAY HOUSE

CCN: 505476 · SEATTLE, WA 98112 · King County

Overview

Address
2720 EAST MADISON, SEATTLE, WA 98112
Phone
2063225300

Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Data not available.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-05 18 17 1 100 1 100
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-20 19 18 1 92 1 92
Total weighted health score
98.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (48)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

48 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-07-16 2025-08-29
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0569 Notify each resident of certain balances and convey resident funds upon discharge, eviction, or death. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0811 Ensure that residents are assessed for appropriateness for a feeding assistant program, receive services as per their plan of care, and feeding assistants are trained and supervised. D 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-05-05 2025-06-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-26 2025-04-01
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. E 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-05-20 2024-06-24
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0729 Verify that a nurse aide has been trained; and if they haven't worked as a nurse aide for 2 years, receive retraining. D 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2023-03-01 2023-04-14
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2023-03-01 2023-04-14

Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2023-04-14 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag
Deficiencies 0811 — correction_date 2025-06-18 0811.correction_date
Deficiencies 0811 — description Ensure that residents are assessed for appropriateness for a feeding assistant program, receive services as per their plan of care, and feeding assistants are trained and supervised. 0811.description
Deficiencies 0811 — scope_severity D 0811.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0811 — survey_date 2025-05-05 0811.survey_date

Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description
Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description

Deficiencies 0887 — description Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. 0887.description
